title: Vector Addition Calculator
description: Industrial-grade vector addition tool for force, velocity, and displacement analysis in manufacturing and logistics. Compliant with ISO 80000-2, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Vector 1 Magnitude
inputs.businessContext: Magnitude of the first force/velocity vector in Newtons (N) or m/s. Typical range for industrial applications: 0.1–100,000 N.

businessContext_i18n.en: Magnitude of the first force/velocity vector in Newtons (N) or m/s. Typical range for industrial applications: 0.1–100,000 N.

inputs.label: Vector 1 Angle
inputs.businessContext: Direction of the first vector measured from positive x-axis (0° to 360°). Used in force analysis per ISO 80000-2.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direction of the first vector measured from positive x-axis (0° to 360°). Used in force analysis per ISO 80000-2.

inputs.label: Vector 2 Magnitude
inputs.businessContext: Magnitude of the second vector. Common in multi-force systems (e.g., conveyor belt tensions, robotic arm loads).

businessContext_i18n.en: Magnitude of the second vector. Common in multi-force systems (e.g., conveyor belt tensions, robotic arm loads).

inputs.label: Vector 2 Angle
inputs.businessContext: Direction of the second vector. Typical angles: 0° (horizontal), 90° (vertical), 180° (opposite).

businessContext_i18n.en: Direction of the second vector. Typical angles: 0° (horizontal), 90° (vertical), 180° (opposite).

inputs.label: Unit System
inputs.businessContext: Select SI (metric) or Imperial (lb, ft/s) for output consistency. ISO 1000 recommends SI for engineering calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select SI (metric) or Imperial (lb, ft/s) for output consistency. ISO 1000 recommends SI for engineering calculations.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input measurement accuracy (e.g., from sensor calibration). Used for Six Sigma risk adjustment.

businessContext_i18n.en: Confidence in input measurement accuracy (e.g., from sensor calibration). Used for Six Sigma risk adjustment.

rules.condition: vector1_angle < 0 OR vector1_angle > 360 OR vector2_angle < 0 OR vector2_angle > 360

rules.message: Confidence level must be between 50% and 100%.
Both vectors are zero. Result will be zero vector.

thresholds.message: Resultant force exceeds 50,000 N. Structural integrity check recommended per ISO 13849.
Vectors are nearly aligned. Check for potential resonance or load concentration.
Low data confidence. Consider recalibrating sensors or increasing sample size (Lean Six Sigma DMAIC).

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
